description: Complete rewrite of HFT_Scalper_Pro.mq5 to exactly match the Python backtest logic from run_aggressive_backtest.py. The EA must produce the same signal frequency (~847 trades / ~30 per day on M1 XAUUSD) as the Python version.
steps: Rewrite RSI calculation to use MT5 built-in iRSI() indicator handles (which use Wilder smoothing internally) for both period 8 and period 14. Create handles in OnInit() and copy values in OnTick(). The Python compute_rsi() uses Wilder smoothing: avg_g = (avg_g * (period - 1) + gains[i]) / period. MT5's iRSI() does the same internally.
Rewrite ATR calculation to use MT5 built-in iATR() indicator handle (period 14, Wilder smoothing). Create handle in OnInit(), copy buffer in OnTick().

Rewrite signal generation to match Python EXACTLY. Three independent signal sources, checked in order with early return: (1) RSI(8) < 25 -> buy, RSI(8) > 75 -> sell; (2) RSI(14) < 30 -> buy, RSI(14) > 70 -> sell; (3) 4-bar reversal: check if last 4 bars each closed lower than the one before (buy) or higher (sell). Each source works independently - if the first one triggers, use that signal without checking others.

Fix 4-bar reversal bar indexing. In Python: close[i-j] < close[i-j-1] for j in range(4) checks bars i, i-1, i-2, i-3 each lower than previous. In MQL5 bar indexing (bar 0 = current), this means: iClose(symbol,tf,1) < iClose(symbol,tf,2) AND iClose(symbol,tf,2) < iClose(symbol,tf,3) AND iClose(symbol,tf,3) < iClose(symbol,tf,4) AND iClose(symbol,tf,4) < iClose(symbol,tf,5) for all_down (4 consecutive bars each closed lower than the bar before it). Note: we check from bar 1 (last completed bar) not bar 0 (current incomplete bar).

Fix session filter to work reliably in MT5 Strategy Tester. In tester, TimeGMT() may not work correctly. Use TimeCurrent() with a broker UTC offset input parameter. The Python uses hours[i] >= 7 and hours[i] <= 20. The EA should extract the hour from the current bar time (iTime) and apply the offset.

Rewrite cooldown logic to match Python exactly. Python tracks last_entry_bars per slot and checks (i - last_entry_bars[s]) >= cooldown (3 bars). In MQL5 on M1, track the bar time of last entry per slot and check that at least 3 bars (3 minutes) have passed. Use bar counting rather than time arithmetic for reliability.

Rewrite two-mode position sizing to exactly match Python: eq_ratio = equity / peak_equity; dd_scale = eq_ratio^dd_power; risk = risk_protect + (risk_grow - risk_protect) * dd_scale; streak boost if consec_wins >= streak_n and dd_scale > 0.8: risk *= streak_mult; cap risk between 0.002 and max_risk_cap (0.25); lot = (equity * risk) / (sl_dist * CONTRACT_SIZE) where CONTRACT_SIZE = SymbolInfoDouble(symbol, SYMBOL_TRADE_CONTRACT_SIZE) which is 100 for XAUUSD.

Match the Python's exact parameter defaults in EA inputs: rsi_entry=25, sl_mult=2.0, tp_mult=3.0, risk_grow=0.17, risk_protect=0.025, dd_power=13, cooldown=3, max_positions=2, session_start=7, session_end=20, dd_halt=14.9%, streak_n=3, streak_mult=1.3, max_risk_cap=0.25, atr_period=14.
Remove the trailing stop feature (the Python backtest does NOT use trailing stops - it only uses fixed SL/TP). Remove the InpUseTrailingStop input and ManageTrailingStops() function.

Remove the max daily loss safety check (not in Python). Remove the max account drawdown emergency stop (not in Python - it only has dd_halt). Remove max spread check (not in Python). Keep ONLY the dd_halt check which is in the Python.
Ensure the EA processes signals on each new M1 bar (not on every tick), matching the Python bar-by-bar processing. The EA should check for a new bar using iTime() comparison.
Set minimum SL/TP distances matching Python: if sl_dist < 0.5: sl_dist = 0.5; if tp_dist < 0.3: tp_dist = 0.3.
Match Python entry price behavior: in Python, entry_price = close[i] +/- slippage. In MT5, entry is at market (Ask for buy, Bid for sell), which naturally includes spread. This is correct as-is.
Keep the trade execution with retry logic, magic number tracking, and position counting - these are necessary for MT5 operation and don't affect signal logic.
Ensure OnTradeTransaction tracks consecutive wins correctly for the streak boost feature.
acceptance_criteria: EA uses iRSI() handles for RSI calculation (Wilder smoothing matching Python)
EA uses iATR() handle for ATR calculation
Signal generation has 3 independent sources checked in priority order: RSI(8), RSI(14), 4-bar reversal
RSI(8) thresholds are <25 for buy, >75 for sell
RSI(14) thresholds are <30 for buy, >70 for sell (matching Python rsi_entry+5 and 95-rsi_entry)
4-bar reversal checks bars 1-5 (completed bars) with correct comparison direction
Session filter uses bar time with UTC offset, not TimeGMT()
Cooldown is bar-count based (3 bars minimum between entries per slot)
Position sizing formula exactly matches Python: lot = (equity * risk) / (sl_dist * contract_size)
No trailing stop (not in Python backtest)
No daily loss limit (not in Python backtest)
No max spread filter (not in Python backtest)
All input parameter defaults match the Python best_params values
verification: Read the final .mq5 file and manually verify each logic block matches the corresponding Python code
Confirm iRSI and iATR handles are created in OnInit() and buffers are copied correctly
Confirm signal generation order matches Python (RSI8 first, RSI14 second, 4-bar third)
Confirm no trailing stop code remains
Confirm no daily loss or max spread checks remain
